steps.title: Split each nucleic acid extract into...
steps.instruction: Split each nucleic acid extract into two subsamples for RNA and DNA virus detection.

steps.title: If required, make up each sample...
steps.instruction: If required, make up each sample to 50 µL with Nuclease-free water.

steps.title: Prepare two 0
steps.instruction: Prepare two 0.2 mL PCR tubes per sample labelled with R (for the RNA pre-treatment) or D (for DNA pre-treatment) along with the sample names. For multiple samples it is recommended that PCR strip tubes or plates are used, ensure that subsamples are grouped for RNA or DNA virus detection and able to be easily separated.

steps.title: Add 25 µL, sample to the...
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL, sample to the tube R and 25 µL, sample to tube D . Subsample R → proceed to RNA virus detection - DNase I treatment (Step 3). Subsample D → proceed to DNA virus detection - Microbiome enrichment (step 14). It is recommended to process the RNA samples first to minimise degradation.

steps.title: Prepare DNase I mix as follows...
steps.instruction: Prepare DNase I mix as follows (for multiple samples prepare a master mix with 10% excess): A B 1 Component Volume (μl) 2 10X DNase I buffer 3 3 DNase I 2 4 Total 5 Ambion™ DNase I (RNase-free) Thermo Fisher Catalog #AM2224 For large amounts of DNA use the suppliers recommendations.

steps.title: Add 5 µL, DNase I mix...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL, DNase I mix to 25 µL, RNA sample .

steps.title: Incubate as follows
steps.instruction: Incubate as follows: 37 °C for 00:15:00 then place On ice .

steps.title: Perform clean-up with 2X volume of RNAclean XP magnetic beads
steps.instruction: Perform clean-up with 2X volume of RNAclean XP magnetic beads. Agencourt RNAClean XP Magnetic Beads Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63987 Ensure RNAclean XP beads are equilibrated to room temperature for ~30 min and vortex well before use.

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Place on a magnetic rack until...
steps.instruction: Place on a magnetic rack until beads and solution have fully separated.

steps.title: Carefully remove supernatant being careful not to disturb the beads
steps.instruction: Carefully remove supernatant being careful not to disturb the beads.

steps.title: Wash 2X with 200 µL, 80% Ethanol
steps.instruction: Wash 2X with 200 µL, 80% Ethanol . Ensure ethanol is freshly prepared.

steps.title: Remove all traces of ethanol and...
steps.instruction: Remove all traces of ethanol and air dry for up to 00:05:00 . Take care not to over dry the beads.

steps.title: To elute DNase I treated RNA...
steps.instruction: To elute DNase I treated RNA add 12 µL, Nuclease-free water and incubate at Room temperature for at least 00:02:00

steps.title: Add 60 µL, RNAclean XP beads...
steps.instruction: Add 60 µL, RNAclean XP beads to the 30 µL, DNase I reaction and mix by pipetting.

steps.title: Transfer 10 µL, DNase I treated RNA to fresh 0
steps.instruction: Transfer 10 µL, DNase I treated RNA to fresh 0.2 mL tubes/plate for Ribo-zero probe hybridisation. Illumina Ribo-Zero Plus rRNA Depletion Kit Illumina, Inc. Catalog #20040526 Ribo-depletion is recommended for sample that are likely to contain large levels of host or bacterial RNA (such as tissue biopsy, faecal, oral or nasal) but can be excluded from sample with lower levels of host RNA (such as plasma, serum or cerebrospinal fluid), or when the sample input is too low to enable library preparation.

steps.title: Thaw DB1 and DP1 at Room...
steps.instruction: Thaw DB1 and DP1 at Room temperature , vortex to mix and centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: Prepare the hybridisation probe mix on...
steps.instruction: Prepare the hybridisation probe mix on ice (for multiple samples prepare a master mix with 10% excess): A B 1 Component Volume (μl) 2 DB1 (Depletion Probe Buffer) 3 3 DP1 (Depletion Probe Pool) 1 4 Total 4

steps.title: Add 4 µL, hybridisation probe mix...
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL, hybridisation probe mix to each 10 µL, sample and fully mix by pipetting 10 times.

steps.title: Incubate samples as follows
steps.instruction: Incubate samples as follows: 95 °C for 00:02:00 Decrease 0.1 °C, /sec until temperature reaches 37 °C then hold. The program takes ∼ 15 min to finish.

steps.title: Prepare rRNA Depletion
steps.instruction: Prepare rRNA Depletion.

steps.title: Thaw RDB and RDE at Room...
steps.instruction: Thaw RDB and RDE at Room temperature , vortex or flick (RDE) to mix and centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: Prepare the rRNA Depletion mix (for...
steps.instruction: Prepare the rRNA Depletion mix (for multiple samples prepare a master mix with 10% excess): A B 1 Component Volume (μl) 2 RDB (RNA Depletion Buffer) 4 3 RDE (RNA Depletion Enzyme) 1 4 Total 5

steps.title: Add 5 µL, rRNA Depletion mix...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL, rRNA Depletion mix to each 14 µL and fully mix by pipetting 10 times.

steps.title: Incubate samples as follows
steps.instruction: Incubate samples as follows: 37 °C for 00:15:00 4 °C, hold

steps.title: Prepare probe removal
steps.instruction: Prepare probe removal.

steps.title: Thaw PRB and PRE at Room...
steps.instruction: Thaw PRB and PRE at Room temperature , vortex (PRB) or flick (PRE) to mix and centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: Prepare the Probe Removal mix On...
steps.instruction: Prepare the Probe Removal mix On ice (for multiple samples prepare a mastermix with 10% excess): A B 1 Component Volume (μl) 2 PRB (Probe Removal Buffer) 7 3 PRE (Probe Removal Enzyme) 3 4 Total 10

steps.title: Add 10 µL, Probe Removal mix...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL, Probe Removal mix to each 19 µL, reaction and fully mix by pipetting 10 times.

steps.title: Incubate samples as follows
steps.instruction: Incubate samples as follows: 37 °C for 00:15:00 70 °C for 00:15:00 4 °C, hold

steps.title: Perform clean-up with 2X volume of RNAclean XP magnetic beads
steps.instruction: Perform clean-up with 2X volume of RNAclean XP magnetic beads. Agencourt RNAClean XP Magnetic Beads Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63987 Ensure RNAclean XP beads are equilibrated to room temperature for ~30 min and vortex well before use.

steps.title: Add 60 µL, RNAClean XP beads...
steps.instruction: Add 60 µL, RNAClean XP beads to the 30 µL, reaction , mix by pipetting.

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00

steps.title: Place on a magnetic rack until...
steps.instruction: Place on a magnetic rack until beads and solution have fully separated.

steps.title: Remove and discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove and discard supernatant.

steps.title: Wash 2X with 175 µL, 80% Ethanol (freshly prepared)
steps.instruction: Wash 2X with 175 µL, 80% Ethanol (freshly prepared) .

steps.title: Remove all traces of ethanol and...
steps.instruction: Remove all traces of ethanol and air dry for up to 00:02:00 Do not over-dry the beads.

steps.title: Elute in 12 µL, ELB (Elution...
steps.instruction: Elute in 12 µL, ELB (Elution buffer) by incubating at Room temperature for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: Transfer 10 µL, RNA sample to fresh 0
steps.instruction: Transfer 10 µL, RNA sample to fresh 0.2 mL tubes/plates. This method for cDNA preparation has been tested for very low-input and undetectable RNA/DNA by Qubit HS reagents.

steps.title: Prepare NTP/Hex mix (for multiple samples...
steps.instruction: Prepare NTP/Hex mix (for multiple samples prepare a master mix with 10% excess): A B 1 Component Volume (μl) 2 10 mM dNTP 1 3 Random Hexamers 1 4 Total 2 Deoxynucleotide (dNTP) Solution Mix New England Biolabs Catalog #N0447S Random hexamers Thermo Scientific Catalog #N8080127

steps.title: Add 2 µL, NTP/Hex mix to each 10 µL, sample
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL, NTP/Hex mix to each 10 µL, sample .

steps.title: Incubate as follows
steps.instruction: Incubate as follows: 65 °C for 00:05:00 immediately place On ice

steps.title: Prepare the SSIII master mix (for...
steps.instruction: Prepare the SSIII master mix (for multiple samples prepare a master mix with 10% excess): A B 1 Component Volume (μl) 2 5X Reverse transcription buffer 4 3 SuperScript III 2 4 RNaseOUT 1 5 DTT 1 6 Total 8 SuperScript™ III Reverse Transcriptase Thermo Fisher Catalog #18080044 RNaseOUT™ Recombinant Ribonuclease Inhibitor Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #10777019

steps.title: Add 8 µL, SSIII mix to each 12 µL, reaction
steps.instruction: Add 8 µL, SSIII mix to each 12 µL, reaction .

steps.title: Prepare second strand mix (for multiple...
steps.instruction: Prepare second strand mix (for multiple samples prepare a mastermix with 10% excess): A B 1 Component Volume (μl) 2 10X Second strand synthesis buffer 8 3 Second strand synthesis enzyme 4 4 Nuclease-free water 48 5 Total 60 NEBNext Ultra II Non-Directional RNA Second Strand Synthesis Module - 100 rxns New England Biolabs Catalog #E6111L

steps.title: Add 60 µL, second strand mix...
steps.instruction: Add 60 µL, second strand mix to each 20 µL, reaction

steps.title: Incubate as follows on PCR machine...
steps.instruction: Incubate as follows on PCR machine 16 °C for 02:30:00 4 °C, hold

steps.title: Incubate samples as follows
steps.instruction: Incubate samples as follows: 25 °C for 00:10:00 55 °C for 01:00:00 70 °C for 00:15:00 4 °C, hold

steps.title: Perform clean up with 1X volume of AmPure XP beads
steps.instruction: Perform clean up with 1X volume of AmPure XP beads. Agencourt AmPure XP beads Contributed by users Catalog #A63880 Ensure AmPure XP beads are equilibrated to room temperature for 30 min and vortex well before use.

steps.title: Add 80 µL, AmPure XP to...
steps.instruction: Add 80 µL, AmPure XP to each 80 µL, reaction (1:1 Ampure:sample ratio) and mix well.

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Place on a magnetic rack until...
steps.instruction: Place on a magnetic rack until beads and solution have fully separated.

steps.title: Remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant.

steps.title: Wash 2X with 200 µL, 80% Ethanol (freshly prepared)
steps.instruction: Wash 2X with 200 µL, 80% Ethanol (freshly prepared) .

steps.title: Remove all traces of ethanol and air dry for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Remove all traces of ethanol and air dry for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Elute in 27 µL, 10 mM...
steps.instruction: Elute in 27 µL, 10 mM Tris pH8 by incubation at Room temperature for 00:02:00 . cDNA can be stored at 4°C overnight or -20°C longer term cDNA → proceed to Section – Library prep (step 22) or store until DNA samples ready so can process together.

steps.title: Pre-bind MBD2-Fc Protein to Magnetic Beads
steps.instruction: Pre-bind MBD2-Fc Protein to Magnetic Beads. NEBNext Microbiome DNA Enrichment Kit - 24 rxns New England Biolabs Catalog #E2612L See following attachment for reagent calculations: Microbiome_calculations.xlsx Microbiome enrichment can be used to deplete CpG modified DNA.

steps.title: Pipette 1 µL, Protein A magnetic...
steps.instruction: Pipette 1 µL, Protein A magnetic beads (see column B of reagent calculation table) for every 6.25 ng, input DNA into a 1.5 mL DNA LoBind tube. For low or undetectable amounts of DNA (<30 ng) use 5 μL of magnetic beads. Do not vortex the magnetic beads.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.1 V, MBD2-Fc protein (see column C of reagent calculation table) to the Protein A magnetic beads.

steps.title: Mix the bead-protein mixture by placing...
steps.instruction: Mix the bead-protein mixture by placing the tube in a rotating mixer for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Prepare the 1X Bind/Wash buffer and keep it On ice
steps.instruction: Prepare the 1X Bind/Wash buffer and keep it On ice : A B 1 Component Volume (μl) 2 5X NEBNext Bind/Wash buffer 800 3 Nuclease-free water 3200 4 Total 4000

steps.title: After the incubation, briefly spin the...
steps.instruction: After the incubation, briefly spin the tube and place on the magnetic rack for 00:05:00 until the beads have collected.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant with a pipette without disturbing the beads
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant with a pipette without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: Add 1 mL, 1X Bind/Wash buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL, 1X Bind/Wash buffer to the tube to wash the beads. Pipet up and down a few times to mix.

steps.title: Mix the beads on a rotating...
steps.instruction: Mix the beads on a rotating mixer for 00:03:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Briefly spin the tube and place...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin the tube and place on the magnetic rack for 00:05:00 until the beads have collected.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant with a pipette without disturbing the beads
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant with a pipette without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: Repeat wash step (2 washes in total)
steps.instruction: Repeat wash step (2 washes in total).

steps.title: Resuspend the beads in the volume...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads in the volume of 1X Bind/Wash buffer equal to the initial magnetic bead volume in step 13.1 (see sum of column B of reagent calculation table).

steps.title: Capture Methylated Host DNA
steps.instruction: Capture Methylated Host DNA.

steps.title: Add appropriate volume of 5X Bind/Wash...
steps.instruction: Add appropriate volume of 5X Bind/Wash buffer to fresh tubes for each sample to give a 1X solution (see column D of reagent calculation table).

steps.title: Add the volume of sample to...
steps.instruction: Add the volume of sample to give up to 1 µg, DNA (see column A of reagent calculation table). It is important that the buffer and DNA is combined before adding the beads! Use a minimum volume of 40 μl and make up with 10 mM Tris pH8 if necessary. Maximum volume is 200 μl.

steps.title: Add appropriate volume of washed Fc-bead/protein...
steps.instruction: Add appropriate volume of washed Fc-bead/protein mix (see column B of reagent calculation table) to the DNA/buffer mix.

steps.title: Mix and incubate in a rotating...
steps.instruction: Mix and incubate in a rotating mixer at RT for 00:15:00 to 04:00:00 depending on sample type . Although the manufacturer's protocol suggests 15 min we would not recommend less than 30 min, and in some cases much longer is required. Ensure that the liquid moves freely during the incubation to achieve efficient mixing.

steps.title: Elute Microbiome DNA
steps.instruction: Elute Microbiome DNA.

steps.title: Briefly centrifuge and place on a...
steps.instruction: Briefly centrifuge and place on a magnet for at least 00:05:00 to separate the bead-bound host DNA.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant containing host depleted/microbiome...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant containing host depleted/microbiome enriched DNA to fresh tubes. The supernatant contains the host-depleted microbiome DNA, the beads contain the microbiome-depleted host DNA. If required the beads can be resuspended in 1X bind buffer and stored for analysis of host DNA see manufacturer's guidelines for details. The supernatant is used directly in the sonication but can be cleaned up with 2X Ampure (see column E of reagent calculation table for volume) for longer term storage. Agencourt AmPure XP beads Contributed by users Catalog #A63880

steps.title: If required make sample up to...
steps.instruction: If required make sample up to 55 µL, with 10mM Tris pH8 .

steps.title: Prepare sonicator for use
steps.instruction: Prepare sonicator for use. Here we describe sonication with the Covaris LE220 and 8microTUBE-50 AFA Fiber Strip V2 or 96 microTUBE AFA Fiber Plate Thin Foil. Be sure to follow the manufacturers recommendations for your own instruments. In addition, some companies offer alternatives to sonication such as the use of fragmentases, this would need to be optimised before use. LE220 (High-throughput focused ultrasonicators, Covaris, 500569, https://www.covaris.com/le220-plus-focused-ultrasonicator-500569 )

steps.title: Fill the tank with water to FILL level -2
steps.instruction: Fill the tank with water to FILL level -2.

steps.title: Switch on the chiller and ensure set to 7 °C
steps.instruction: Switch on the chiller and ensure set to 7 °C .

steps.title: Switch on the water conditioning system,...
steps.instruction: Switch on the water conditioning system, the Covaris and the computer.

steps.title: Open the SonoLab software
steps.instruction: Open the SonoLab software.

steps.title: Select Home and the transducer will...
steps.instruction: Select Home and the transducer will get submerged, the degas pump should start automatically.

steps.title: Degas the water bath for ~ 00:45:00
steps.instruction: Degas the water bath for ~ 00:45:00 .

steps.title: Set up the sonication conditions as follows
steps.instruction: Set up the sonication conditions as follows: A B 1 Peak power 450 2 Duty factor 10 3 Cycles/burst 1000 4 Treatment time (s) 89 5 Dithering on Example sonication conditions to achieve ~350 bp fragments. The precise conditions will need to be validated using a gDNA control prior to use with samples.

steps.title: Sonicate samples
steps.instruction: Sonicate samples.

steps.title: Add 55 µL, Sample to either...
steps.instruction: Add 55 µL, Sample to either the 8 microTUBE-50 AFA Fiber Strip V2 or 96 microTUBE AFA Fiber Plate Thin Foil.

steps.title: Place the strip/plate in the appropriate...
steps.instruction: Place the strip/plate in the appropriate holder and screw into place (ensure that it is set up the same as the program conditions).

steps.title: Select Load position to move the support arm forward
steps.instruction: Select Load position to move the support arm forward.

steps.title: Press the green button and open...
steps.instruction: Press the green button and open the door to put the holder into the support arm, ensure in the correct orientation and close the door.

steps.title: Press start position to submerge the...
steps.instruction: Press start position to submerge the rack and confrim the correct volume of water has been added (samples should NOT be fully submerged).

steps.title: Press Run to start the sonication
steps.instruction: Press Run to start the sonication.

steps.title: Once completed press Load Position to remove the plate
steps.instruction: Once completed press Load Position to remove the plate.

steps.title: Transfer 50 µL, sheared DNA to PCR tubes
steps.instruction: Transfer 50 µL, sheared DNA to PCR tubes. Option to check shearing by running 1 μL on TapeStation gDNA. Genomic DNA ScreenTape Agilent Technologies Catalog #5067-5365 Genomic DNA Reagents Agilent Technologies Catalog #5067-5366 4200 TapeStation System (Electrophoresis tool for DNA and RNA sample quality control., TapeStation Instruments, G2991AA, https://www.agilent.com/en/product/automated-electrophoresis/tapestation-systems/tapestation-instruments/4200-tapestation-system-228263 )
1.4X Ampure clean up. Agencourt AmPure XP beads Contributed by users Catalog #A63880

steps.title: Add 70 µL, Ampure XP to the samples (ratio 1
steps.instruction: Add 70 µL, Ampure XP to the samples (ratio 1.4:1).

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:15:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:15:00 .

steps.title: Place on a magnetic rack until...
steps.instruction: Place on a magnetic rack until beads and solution have fully separated.

steps.title: Remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant.

steps.title: Wash 2X with 200 µL, 80% Ethanol (freshly prepared)
steps.instruction: Wash 2X with 200 µL, 80% Ethanol (freshly prepared) .

steps.title: Remove all traces of Ethanol
steps.instruction: Remove all traces of Ethanol. Air dry for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Elute samples in 25 µL, 10mM Tris pH8
steps.instruction: Elute samples in 25 µL, 10mM Tris pH8 .

steps.title: Transfer 25 µL, sample to new tubes. Pause
steps.instruction: Transfer 25 µL, sample to new tubes. Pause: DNA can be stored at 4°C overnight or -20°C for longer term. Sheared DNA → proceed to Section – Library prep (step 22) can process alongside the prepared cDNA samples.

steps.title: Prepare the End Repair mix (for...
steps.instruction: Prepare the End Repair mix (for multiple samples prepare a master mix with 10% excess): A B 1 Component Volume (μl) 2 10X End repair buffer 3 3 End repair enzyme 2 4 Total 5 This version uses the KAPA LTP kit that has been discontinued by the supplier. Reactions at at half the volume of the manufacturer's recommendations with the final PCR reaction being at full volume. Samples pre-treated for the detection of DNA and RNA viruses can be processed alongside each other. Treat as seperate samples and do not pool before library prep. KAPA LTP library prep kit Roche Catalog #796188001

steps.title: Add 5 µL, End repair mix...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL, End repair mix to each 25 µL, sample .

steps.title: Incubate as follows (if using a...
steps.instruction: Incubate as follows (if using a PCR machine do not use hot lid): 20 °C for 00:30:00
1.4X Ampure XP clean up. Agencourt AmPure XP beads Contributed by users Catalog #A63880

steps.title: Add 20 µL, 10 mM Tris...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL, 10 mM Tris pH8 increase volume of each sample to a total of 50 µL .

steps.title: Add 70 µL, Ampure XP (1
steps.instruction: Add 70 µL, Ampure XP (1.4:1 Ampure:sample ratio). Pipette up and down to mix.

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Place samples on a magnetic rack...
steps.instruction: Place samples on a magnetic rack until beads and solution have fully separated.

steps.title: Remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant.

steps.title: Wash 2X with 200 µL, 80% Ethanol (freshly prepared)
steps.instruction: Wash 2X with 200 µL, 80% Ethanol (freshly prepared) .

steps.title: Remove all traces of ethanol and air dry for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Remove all traces of ethanol and air dry for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Elute in 21 µL, 10mM Tris...
steps.instruction: Elute in 21 µL, 10mM Tris pH8 leaving beads in solution.

13. Prepare the A-Tail mix (for multiple samples prepare a master mix with 10% excess): A B 1 Component Volume (μl) 2 10X A-Tail buffer 2.5 3 A-Tail enzyme 1.5 4 Total 4 KAPA LTP library prep kit Roche Catalog #796188001

steps.title: Add 4 µL, A-Tail mix to each 21 µL, reaction
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL, A-Tail mix to each 21 µL, reaction .

steps.title: Incubate as follows
steps.instruction: Incubate as follows: 30 °C for 01:00:00

1.4X SPRI clean up. SPRI is a solution containing 20% PEG 8000 and 2.5 M NaCl and allows for Ampure clean up using the beads contained within the reaction. SPRI is included as art of the KAPA LTP library prep kit but if required additional SPRI can be made by mixing equal quantities of 40% PEG 8000 and 5 M NaCl. 40% Polyethylene Glycol MW 8000 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P1458 5 M Sodium chloride (NaCl) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #S5150-1L

steps.title: Add 25 µL, 10 mM Tris...
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL, 10 mM Tris pH8 to increase volume of each sample to a total of 50 µL .

steps.title: Add 70 µL, SPRI (1
steps.instruction: Add 70 µL, SPRI (1.4:1 SPRI:sample ratio). Pipette up and down to mix. The magnetic beads should remain in solution form the previous step, alternatively Ampure XP can be used instead of SPRI reagent.

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Place the samples on a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the samples on a magnetic rack until beads and solution have fully separated.

steps.title: Remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant.

steps.title: Wash 2X with 200 µL, 80% Ethanol (freshly prepred)
steps.instruction: Wash 2X with 200 µL, 80% Ethanol (freshly prepred) .

steps.title: Remove all traces of ethanol and air dry for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Remove all traces of ethanol and air dry for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Elute in 15 µL, 10 mM...
steps.instruction: Elute in 15 µL, 10 mM Tris pH8 leaving beads in solution.

steps.title: Quantify 1 µL using Qubit high sensitivity dsDNA
steps.instruction: Quantify 1 µL using Qubit high sensitivity dsDNA. Qubit™ dsDNA HS Assay Kit Invitrogen - Thermo Fisher Catalog #Q32851 Qubit (Flurometer, Invitrogen, Q33228, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/Q33228 , https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/9de3sqe.jpeg )

steps.title: Calculate the amount of pmol per...
steps.instruction: Calculate the amount of pmol per in each 14 µL, A-tail reaction as follows (alternatively use the calculation in the attached sheet): Sample (pmol)=\frac{reaction (ng)}{size(bp)*660}*1000 If the fragment size is not known estimate it to be 350 bp. LTP_adapterCalcultion.xlsx

steps.title: Prepare the Ligation mix (for multiple...
steps.instruction: Prepare the Ligation mix (for multiple samples prepare a master mix with 10% excess): A B 1 Component Volume (μl) 2 5X ligation buffer 5 3 DNA ligase 2.5 4 Total 7.5 KAPA LTP library prep kit Roche Catalog #796188001

steps.title: Add 7
steps.instruction: Add 7.5 µL, Ligation mix to 14 µL, reaction (ensure remains on the beads).

steps.title: Add 5 µL, diluted NEBNext adapter
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL, diluted NEBNext adapter .

steps.title: Incubate as follows (if using a...
steps.instruction: Incubate as follows (if using a PCR machine ensure the hot lid is not turned on): 20 °C for 01:00:00 .

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.75 µL, USER enzyme to each tube. USER enzyme is included as part of the NEBNext multiplex oligo kit. NEBNext Multiplex Oligos for Illumina (96 Unique Dual Index Primer Pairs) New England Biolabs Catalog #E6440-8
0.9X SPRI clean up.

steps.title: Add 25 µL, 10 mM Tris...
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL, 10 mM Tris pH8 to make volume up to 50 µL .

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Place on a magnetic rack until...
steps.instruction: Place on a magnetic rack until beads and solution have fully separated.

steps.title: Remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant.

steps.title: Wash 2X with 200 µL, 80% Ethanol (freshly prepared)
steps.instruction: Wash 2X with 200 µL, 80% Ethanol (freshly prepared) .

steps.title: Remove all traces of ethanol and...
steps.instruction: Remove all traces of ethanol and air dry for up to 00:05:00 . Take care not to over-dry the beads.

steps.title: Elute samples in 22 µL, 10 mM Tris pH8
steps.instruction: Elute samples in 22 µL, 10 mM Tris pH8 .

steps.title: Transfer 20 µL to new freah 0
steps.instruction: Transfer 20 µL to new freah 0.2 mL PCR tubes.

steps.title: Add 5 µL, NEBNext multiplex oligos each sample
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL, NEBNext multiplex oligos each sample. It is essential the UDI primers are used to prevent index hopping on the sequencer. It is recommended that the primer kits are rotated between batches, there are currently 5 NEBNext multiplex oligo kits. NEBNext Multiplex Oligos for Illumina (96 Unique Dual Index Primer Pairs) New England Biolabs Catalog #E6440-8

steps.title: Add 25 µL, KAPA HotStart Ready mix to each sample
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL, KAPA HotStart Ready mix to each sample. KAPA HotStart Ready mix is contained within the KAPA LTP kit but may need to be topped up with additional reagent. KAPA LTP library prep kit Roche Catalog #796188001 HotStart ReadyMix (KAPA HiFi PCR kit) Kapa Biosystems Catalog #KK2601

steps.title: Incubate as follows
steps.instruction: Incubate as follows: 98 °C for 00:00:45 4-20* cycles of 98 °C for 00:00:15 65 °C, ** for 00:00:30 72 °C for 00:00:30 Final cycle of 72 °C for 00:01:00 4 °C, hold 4oC hold *The number of cycles depends on the input DNA concentration, too few cycles and not enough library is produced, too many results in a high proportion of PCR duplicates and daisy chains. A B C 1 Input DNA (into ER and AT) Number of cycles required to generate: 2 100 ng library 1 μg library 3 1 μg 3 3 4 500 ng 3 3–4 5 250 ng 3 4–6 6 100 ng 3 6–7 7 50 ng 3–5 7–8 8 25 ng 5–7 8 – 10 9 10 ng 7–9 11 – 13 10 5 ng 9 – 11 13 – 14 11 2.5 ng 11 – 13 14 – 16 12 1 ng 13 – 15 17 – 19 KAPA recommendations for number of PCR cycles required for different amounts of input DNA. As the PCR primers contain the full Illumina adapter/index sequences at least 3 cycles are required. **The annealing temperature of 65°C is optimal for the NEBNext primers, if other primers are used this may need to be altered. It is recommended that after this step (i.e. post-PCR when samples are more concentrated and contain common adapters) work continues in a different area to prevent contamination.

steps.title: Calculate the amount of adapter required...
steps.instruction: Calculate the amount of adapter required (aim for 20:1 ratio adapter to sample - see calculation sheet). The adapter is contained in the NEBNext multiplex oligo kit. NEBNext Multiplex Oligos for Illumina (96 Unique Dual Index Primer Pairs) New England Biolabs Catalog #E6440-8

steps.title: Dilute adapter in water to achieve...
steps.instruction: Dilute adapter in water to achieve appropriate concentration in a total volume of 5 µL per reaction. If DNA is undetectable by Qubit HS reagents use 5 μL of 0.15 μM adapter.

steps.title: Step 15 Incubate as follows
steps.instruction: Step 15 Incubate as follows: 37 °C for 00:15:00 4 °C, hold

steps.title: Add 45 µL, SPRI to the samples (ratio 0
steps.instruction: Add 45 µL, SPRI to the samples (ratio 0.9:1).

steps.title: Clean up and QC the libraries...
steps.instruction: Clean up and QC the libraries as in protocol Library clean up and quality control for Illumina sequencing .

steps.title: Metagenomic sequencing can speed up identification...
steps.instruction: Metagenomic sequencing can speed up identification of any viruses in the samples and, depending on the virus and viral load, may be suficient to generate full genomes.

steps.title: Pool the libraries by equal molarity...
steps.instruction: Pool the libraries by equal molarity and QC the pools as described in the protocol Library pooling and quality control for Illumina sequencing . Ensure that enough unpooled library remains to perform targeted enrichment .

steps.title: Sequence the pools on an Illumina...
steps.instruction: Sequence the pools on an Illumina sequencer following the manufacturer's guidelines. For metagenomic viral discovery sequencing we recommend sequencing at 40 million reads per sample (20 million for the RNA viral detection and 20 million for the DNA viral detection).

steps.title: Using the bp size and ng/μl...
steps.instruction: Using the bp size and ng/μl concentration calculate the nM concentration for each library as follows: Conc (nM) = \frac {sample (ng/μl)}{size (bp) * 660 (g/mol)}*1000000

steps.title: Thoroughly pipette mix
steps.instruction: Thoroughly pipette mix.

steps.title: Thoroughly pipette mix
steps.instruction: Thoroughly pipette mix.

steps.title: Thoroughly pipette mix
steps.instruction: Thoroughly pipette mix.

steps.title: Quantify the DNA and RNA concentration...
steps.instruction: Quantify the DNA and RNA concentration of your samples using Qubit HS reagents. Qubit™ dsDNA HS Assay Kit Invitrogen - Thermo Fisher Catalog #Q32851 Qubit RNA HS Assay Kit Invitrogen - Thermo Fisher Catalog #Q32852 Qubit (Flurometer, Invitrogen, Q33228, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/Q33228 , https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/9de3sqe.jpeg ) Depending on the sample type the level of RNA/DNA may be undetectable and so quantification may not be required for all samples.
